channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump wants a Gaza ceasefire deal 'now,' Graham says
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) said that President-elect Trump wants to reach a ceasefire and hostage deal before inauguration day on Jan. 20, Axios reported on Friday. "Trump is more determined than ever to release the hostages and supports a ceasefire that includes a hostage deal.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: What to know about sudden rebel gains in Syria's 13-year war and why it matters
item.description: The 13-year civil war in Syria has roared back into prominence with a surprise rebel offensive on one of Syria’s largest cities and ancient business hub.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ireland votes in a close-run election where incumbents hope to cling on to power
item.description: Ireland is voting in a parliamentary election that will determine the country's next government; voters are selecting more than 170 lawmakers to sit in the Dail.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: 2 children and a woman crushed to death outside Gaza bakery amid food shortage
item.description: Two children and a woman were crushed to death Friday as a crowd of Palestinians pushed to get bread at a bakery in the Gaza Strip amid a worsening food crisis in the war-ravaged territory, medical officials said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: British lawmakers give initial approval to a bill to allow terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British lawmakers gave initial approval on Friday to a bill to help terminally ill adults end their lives in England and Wales, following an impassioned debate that saw people sharing personal stories of loss and suffering.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nigeria confirms that at least 27 people died and more than 100 are missing after boat capsized
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — At least 27 people died and more than 100, mostly women, were missing on Friday, after a boat transporting them to a food market capsized along the River Niger in northern Nigeria, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Trudeau flies to Florida to meet with Trump on tariffs threat
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau has flown to Florida to have dinner with President-elect Donald Trump at his Mar-a-Lago club after Trump threatened to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: School bus driver charged with holding girl captive for 12 years
item.description: A school bus driver in Colombia allegedly held a girl captive for more than a decade until she recently escaped, prosecutors say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK spy chief says Russia waging 'reckless' sabotage campaign in Europe
item.description: The head of Britain's Secret Intelligence Service said Russia's war against Ukraine is part of a “staggeringly reckless campaign of Russian sabotage” aimed at Europe.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israel cease-fire deal has critics worried over Biden, Obama officials looking to stymie incoming Trump admin
item.description: After 14 months of war between Israel and Hezbollah, a cease-fire went into effect this week amid allegations that the truce happened due to pressure put on Israel despite denials from the administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Notre Dame Cathedral unveils its new interior 5 years after devastating fire
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After more than five years of frenetic, but sometimes interrupted, reconstruction work, Notre Dame Cathedral showed itself anew to the world Friday, with rebuilt soaring ceilings and creamy good-as-new stonework erasing somber memories of its devastating fire in 2019.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy ordered missile strikes into Russia hours after trip to front lines with Fox News
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy recently discussed the latest developments with his country's war against Russia with Fox News chief foreign correspondent Trey Yingst.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK intelligence chief accuses Russia of 'staggeringly reckless' sabotage campaign
item.description: The head of Britain’s foreign intelligence service says Russia is conducting a “staggeringly reckless” sabotage campaign against Ukraine's allies that risks dangerously escalating the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In a shock offensive, insurgents breach Syria's largest city for the first time since 2016
item.description: Insurgents have breached Syria’s largest city and clashed with government forces for the first time since 2016.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Georgian protesters clash with police for a second night after EU talks are suspended
item.description: Thousands of demonstrators protesting the Georgian government's decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union have clashed with police outside the parliament building for a second straight night.
item.dc:creator: Sophiko Megrelidze, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ukrainian energy workers carry out repairs despite Russia's pounding of the country's power grid
item.description: Workers at one of Ukraine's thermal power plants struggle to repair damage and keep electricity flowing as Russia continues its campaign of attacks targeting the country's power grid.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ireland headed for coalition government following parliamentary election, exit poll suggests
item.description: An exit poll in Ireland’s general election suggests the three biggest parties have won roughly equal shares and the country is headed for another coalition government.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless And Peter Morrison, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Embattled Japan leader vows to cooperate with long-ignored opposition in bid to save his government
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Embattled Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, who survived as leader after a major election loss by his governing coalition, promised Friday to work closely with the long-ignored opposition that is now the only way his minority government can stay in power.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia suspends talks on joining the European Union and accuses the bloc of blackmail
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Georgia is suspending talks on its bid to join the European Union for four years, its prime minister said Thursday, in view of what he described as “blackmail and manipulation” from some of the bloc's politicians.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia and Syria bomb Syrian Islamist rebels after surprise incursion
item.description: Syrian rebels launch attack against Hezbollah’s ally, Syria’s regime, amid Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Avocados, tequila and other iconic Mexican products are jeopardized by Trump's tariff threats
item.description: URUAPAN, Mexico (AP) — Mexicans are worried that threats by Donald Trump to impose 25% tariffs could affect a wide range of iconic Mexican products and threaten entire regional economies. In western Mexico, no crop supplies an income for so many small growers as avocados.
item.dc:creator: ARMANDO SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel opens fire in Lebanon at ‘suspects’ allegedly violating truce, which has entered its second day
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces opened fire Thursday on "suspects" in southern Lebanon who allegedly are violating the terms of the ceasefire deal with Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden admin urges Ukraine to lower draft age to conscript 18-year-olds
item.description: The Biden administration is urging Ukraine to lower its draft age to conscript 18-year-olds to keep pace with Russia’s growing military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia launches another large missile, drone attack on Ukraine's energy infrastructure
item.description: Russia launched another attack on Ukraine's energy infrastructure on Thursday, leaving over a million households without power and forcing emergency power to be implemented nationwide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump taps retired General Keith Kellogg for Ukraine envoy role
item.description: While there is currently no special envoy for the war in Ukraine, Trump had privately expressed interest in creating the position.
item.dc:creator: Gram Slattery, Svea Herbst-Bayliss and Steve Holland / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Australian police officer who used Taser on 95-year-old woman found guilty of manslaughter
item.description: A police officer who shocked a 95-year-old nursing home resident with a Taser was found guilty of manslaughter in an Australian court, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: World's oldest man has died in England at 112
item.description: John Alfred Tinniswood was born a few months after the sinking of the Titanic and lived through two world wars.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump pledges Mexico, Canada, China tariffs over border, fentanyl
item.description: "Thousands of people are pouring through Mexico and Canada, bringing Crime and Drugs at levels never seen before," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Drones detected near 3 US bases in England last week: Air Force
item.description: Between Wednesday and Friday, multiple unidentified drones were detected near RAF Lakenheath, RAF Mildenhall and RAF Feltwell.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9
